skill.name: pathology-roi-selector
skill.description: Identifies regions of interest (tumor, stroma, necrosis, lymphocyte) in whole slide pathology images for AI training data preparation.
skill.summary: Auto-detect tissue regions in pathology whole slide images for research and AI model training.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 36 potential issues, all evaluated as false positives after manual review. The 21 external_commands findings correspond to bash code blocks in SKILL.md documentation, not active code execution. The 3 filesystem findings are either a security checklist (path traversal references) or legitimate file output (main.py line 41 exports ROI coordinates to user-specified path). No weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, or network exfiltration was confirmed. The skill is a benign Python script with no external API calls and no obfuscated behavior.

low_findings.title: Python file write for ROI export
low_findings.description: Script writes ROI coordinates to a user-specified output JSON file. This is expected and documented behavior for the pathology ROI selector tool. The output path is controlled by the --output argument.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File write is intentional functionality for exporting results. Output path is user-specified via command-line argument with no hardcoded paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command examples in documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md contains bash code blocks showing example commands to compile and run the Python script. These are documentation-only patterns and are not executed by the AI agent. All commands reference the skill's own scripts directory.

content.user_title: Detect regions of interest in pathology whole slide images
content.value_statement: Pathology researchers need to identify tumor and tissue regions in large whole slide images for AI training data. This skill automates ROI detection from WSI files and exports structured coordinates for downstream analysis.
actual_capabilities: Detect tumor, stroma, necrosis, and lymphocyte regions from whole slide images
Filter detected ROIs by confidence threshold for quality control
Export ROI coordinates and metadata to structured JSON format
Process WSI files through command-line interface with configurable parameters
Document assumptions, scope limits, and output boundaries in results
limitations: Uses placeholder mock detection logic, not a real WSI analysis model
Does not support multi-resolution pyramid image formats
No visualization or overlay output for reviewed ROIs
Requires user to provide valid WSI file paths accessible to the script

use_cases.title: Prepare AI training data from pathology slides
use_cases.description: Extract tumor-rich regions from WSI files to build training datasets for deep learning models in computational pathology.

use_cases.title: Create tissue microarrays from whole slide images
use_cases.description: Select representative tissue regions from multiple WSI files to construct tissue microarray blocks for research studies.

use_cases.title: Sample regions for pathology education
use_cases.description: Identify and export diverse tissue regions (tumor, normal, stroma) from teaching slide collections for educational materials.

prompt_templates.title: Basic ROI detection from a single WSI file
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the pathology ROI selector on the whole slide image at [WSI_FILE_PATH]. Detect tumor regions and export the coordinates to [OUTPUT_PATH].
prompt_templates.scenario: A pathologist needs to quickly identify tumor regions from a single slide image

prompt_templates.title: Batch processing multiple tissue types
prompt_templates.prompt: Process [WSI_FILE_PATH] and detect all available ROI types: tumor, stroma, necrosis, and lymphocyte. Export each type to a separate JSON file. Filter results to keep only ROIs with confidence above 0.85.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs comprehensive tissue region mapping across multiple tissue types

prompt_templates.title: Quality-controlled ROI selection for AI training
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the pathology ROI selector on [WSI_FILE_PATH] for tumor regions. Apply a minimum confidence filter of 0.9. Document the number of ROIs found, their coordinates, and any regions that were filtered out due to low confidence.
prompt_templates.scenario: An AI engineer needs high-confidence training samples with documented quality metrics

prompt_templates.title: Comparative analysis across multiple slides
prompt_templates.prompt: Process all WSI files in [DIRECTORY_PATH] using the pathology ROI selector. For each slide, detect tumor and stroma regions. Export the results with the slide name in each output filename. Summarize the total number of ROIs found across all slides.
prompt_templates.scenario: A research team needs region data from a cohort of pathology slides for a study

output_examples.input: Identify tumor regions from /data/patient_01_wsi.svs with minimum confidence 0.85
Run pathology ROI selector on the attached slide file and export all tissue type results

best_practices: Always validate the WSI file path exists before running the script to avoid file not found errors
Set a confidence threshold between 0.8 and 0.95 to balance recall and precision for your use case
Document input assumptions and output boundaries explicitly in the final deliverable
anti_patterns: Do not run the script on non-pathology image formats such as standard photographs or radiology DICOM files
Do not skip the confidence filtering step when preparing data for clinical or production AI training
Do not assume the mock detection results reflect real pathology analysis without replacing the detection logic

faq.question: What image formats does the pathology ROI selector support?
faq.answer: The script accepts any file path as the --image argument but is designed for whole slide image formats. The detection logic is currently a placeholder and does not perform real image analysis.

faq.question: Does this skill make external API calls or send data over the network?
faq.answer: No. The pathology ROI selector runs entirely locally. It reads input files, processes them, and writes output files. There are no external API calls or data exfiltration risks.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for clinical diagnosis?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed for research and AI training data preparation only. The detection logic is a placeholder and should not be used for clinical decision making.

faq.question: How do I customize the ROI types detected?
faq.answer: The script currently supports four ROI types: tumor, stroma, necrosis, and lymphocyte. You can select the type using the --type argument. The detection logic in PathologyROISelector needs to be replaced with real image analysis for production use.

faq.question: What happens if the output file already exists?
faq.answer: The script will overwrite the existing file without warning. Use a unique output path for each run or implement file naming conventions to avoid data loss.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It provides structured execution with explicit assumptions, bounded scope, and reproducible output format for AI-assisted workflow.
